page.primary_keyword: Marbella hen party yacht charter activities
page.title: Top Hen Party Yacht Charter Activities in Marbella

data.meta_description: Discover the best Marbella hen party yacht charter activities in Puerto Banús. From jet skiing to sunset cruises, our fleet of 10+ boats makes your hen do unforgettable.
data.summary: This guide covers the top activities for a hen party yacht charter in Marbella, including jet skiing, swimming, sunset cruises, and party stops. With boats departing from Puerto Banús, we highlight what makes each activity special for a bachelorette celebration.
key_facts: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (beer, cava, wine, soft drinks), and snacks.
Fleet of 18 boats, from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts, all departing Puerto Banús.
Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a free jet ski on 4-hour+ bookings.
Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive.
Popular hen party stops: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino, and Estepona coast.
Sunset cruises run from 18:00 to 20:00, ideal for photos with La Concha backdrop.
Maximum capacity per boat ranges from 5 to 12 passengers, so plan group size.
IVA (21%) included in all quoted prices – no hidden fees.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a hen party in Marbella?
faq.a: For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, €4,719 minimum 4h) includes a free jet ski – perfect for party fun. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge for socialising. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) is a budget-friendly option.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food, cake, and decorations (balloons, banners, etc.) on any charter. Our crew will help set up. However, glass bottles are not recommended for safety – use plastic cups provided.

faq.q: How far in advance should we book a hen party yacht?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 2–4 weeks ahead, especially for weekends in June–September. Peak season (July–August) often sees Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 booked weeks in advance. Last-minute availability exists but is limited.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on our booking day?
faq.a: If the captain deems conditions unsafe (high winds, storms), we reschedule at no cost or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24–48 hours before departure. Most days in Marbella are calm, especially morning charters.

faq.q: Is there a DJ or music system on the boats?
faq.a: All our boats have Bluetooth stereo systems – you can connect your own playlist. For larger parties, you can request a portable speaker upgrade (free). We do not provide a DJ, but you can hire one independently; let us know in advance.

faq.q: Can we stop for swimming or snorkelling during the charter?
faq.a: Absolutely. The skipper will anchor at calm spots like Cala del Faro (10 min from Puerto Banús) or Cala Cortés (15 min). We provide snorkelling masks on request. Swimming stops are included in all charters – just tell the crew your preference.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for a hen party yacht charter?
faq.a: There is no minimum age for passengers, but all guests must be able to follow safety instructions. Children under 12 must wear life jackets (provided). The minimum age for alcohol consumption in Spain is 18 – ID may be checked.

faq.q: How do we get to the boat in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: All boats depart from Puerto Banús marina, easily reachable by taxi (€15–20 from Marbella centre) or car (free parking at the nearby public car park). We meet you at the designated pontoon – exact location sent via WhatsApp on booking.

data.body_html:
Planning a hen party in Marbella? A private yacht charter is the ultimate way to celebrate the bride-to-be with sun, sea, and style. Departing from glamorous Puerto Banús, our fleet offers everything from sleek speedboats to superyachts, all with a licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included. Whether you want a party cruise, a jet ski adrenaline hit, or a sunset photo session with La Concha mountain as your backdrop, here are the top Marbella hen party yacht charter activities to make your hen do unforgettable.

1. Jet Ski Thrills on the Mangusta 80

For a hen party that craves speed and laughs, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) comes with a free jet ski on any 4-hour+ booking. Take turns riding the waves off Cabopino or Estepona – the skipper will guide you to calm, safe areas. At €4,719 for 4 hours (including jet ski, drinks, and snacks), it’s the top choice for groups of 10–12 who want both luxury and adrenaline.

2. Sunset Cruise with Cava and Canapés

Nothing beats a golden-hour sail along the Marbella coastline. Book a 2-hour sunset slot (18:00–20:00) on the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) or the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h). Watch the sun dip behind La Concha while sipping cava and nibbling snacks. The flybridge offers panoramic views – perfect for group photos. This is the most popular hen party activity for a relaxed, elegant vibe.

3. Swimming and Snorkelling Stops

All our charters include stops for swimming. The skipper will anchor at Cala del Faro (a quiet cove 10 minutes from Puerto Banús) or Cala Cortés (15 minutes east). We provide snorkelling masks on request. The water in summer averages 22–26°C, so it’s ideal for a refreshing dip between drinks. For a longer stop, head to Cabopino – a sandy beach with a chiringuito (beach bar) you can swim to.

4. Party Playlist and Dance Deck

Every boat has a Bluetooth stereo system – create a hen party playlist and blast it through the speakers. Boats like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) and Mangusta 80 (White) have spacious sundecks perfect for dancing. Bring your own decorations (balloons, banners) and the crew will help set them up. No DJ included, but you can hire one – just let us know in advance.

5. Photo Shoot with Marbella’s Best Backdrops

The bride-to-be deserves Instagram-worthy shots. The Golden Mile skyline, Puerto Banús harbour, and the distant La Concha mountain provide stunning backdrops. The Mangusta 80 (Grey) with its sleek lines and panoramic windows is a favourite for photo sessions. Many hen parties bring a photographer – the crew can adjust the route to stop at the most photogenic spots.

6. Lunch or Dinner Cruise to Estepona

Combine sailing with dining. Charter the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) for a 4–6 hour cruise to Estepona (15 NM west). Anchor near the marina and enjoy a meal you bring aboard – we provide plates, cutlery, and a cooler. Alternatively, order tapas from a local catering service (€25–40/person) and we’ll arrange delivery to the pontoon before departure.

7. Hen Party Games on the Bow

The spacious bow of the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94 is perfect for hen party games – think truth or dare, Mr & Mrs quizzes, or a scavenger hunt. The crew can suggest games and provide props (cards, pens) on request. With the Mediterranean breeze and unlimited drinks, the bow becomes your private party zone.

8. Customisable Duration and Extras

All our charters are flexible. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, ideal for a quick celebration. For a full day (8 hours), the price is €2,299. Want to add a water slide? A paddleboard? A photographer? We can arrange most extras – just ask via WhatsApp when booking. All prices include IVA (21%), so no surprises.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for a hen party in Marbella?

For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, €4,719 minimum 4h) includes a free jet ski – perfect for party fun. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge for socialising. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) is a budget-friendly option.

Can we bring our own food and decorations on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food, cake, and decorations (balloons, banners, etc.) on any charter. Our crew will help set up. However, glass bottles are not recommended for safety – use plastic cups provided.

How far in advance should we book a hen party yacht?

We recommend booking at least 2–4 weeks ahead, especially for weekends in June–September. Peak season (July–August) often sees Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 booked weeks in advance. Last-minute availability exists but is limited.

What happens if the weather is bad on our booking day?

If the captain deems conditions unsafe (high winds, storms), we reschedule at no cost or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24–48 hours before departure. Most days in Marbella are calm, especially morning charters.

Is there a DJ or music system on the boats?

All our boats have Bluetooth stereo systems – you can connect your own playlist. For larger parties, you can request a portable speaker upgrade (free). We do not provide a DJ, but you can hire one independently; let us know in advance.

Can we stop for swimming or snorkelling during the charter?

Absolutely. The skipper will anchor at calm spots like Cala del Faro (10 min from Puerto Banús) or Cala Cortés (15 min). We provide snorkelling masks on request. Swimming stops are included in all charters – just tell the crew your preference.

What is the minimum age for a hen party yacht charter?

There is no minimum age for passengers, but all guests must be able to follow safety instructions. Children under 12 must wear life jackets (provided). The minimum age for alcohol consumption in Spain is 18 – ID may be checked.

How do we get to the boat in Puerto Banús?

All boats depart from Puerto Banús marina, easily reachable by taxi (€15–20 from Marbella centre) or car (free parking at the nearby public car park). We meet you at the designated pontoon – exact location sent via WhatsApp on booking.
